notes: This pass continued from the repo-forward seam at C3:2600 using the same caller-plus-local-structure discipline as the recent conservative C3 pages.

The page produced several visible raw targets, but much of the apparent support was fake; the clearest example is C3:0EBA -> C3:2620, where the caller sits inside obvious ASCII/text material rather than executable code.
Multiple higher-bank-looking callers into 2625, 263F, 265D, and 26A5 also come from byte neighborhoods that read more like table/script-style inline data than trustworthy code.

The caller side is better for 2600, 2629, and 26D0 because visible same-bank callers at C3:156D, C3:16A8, and C3:09AF look more code-like, but those targets still fail local byte-level sanity checks and are not promoted.

The strongest unsupported local coherence in the page is a late island around 2678..268F ending at the page's lone RTS, but it has no caller-backed true start and still sits inside mixed-content surroundings.

Because the caller-backed targets are structurally bad and the structurally better late RTS island is unsupported, the whole 2600..26FF page is frozen honestly as mixed inline content before the next 2700 seam.
